headline: Tesla Cybertruck Sales Were Inflated by a SpaceX Buying Spree
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Sales of Tesla Inc.’s Cybertruck have been propped up in recent months by Elon Musk’s other companies, an unusual arrangement that further indicates the polarizing pickup is failing to appeal to everyday buyers.SpaceX, the Musk-led rocket and satellite maker, accounted for 1,279 — or more than 18% — of the 7,071 Cybertrucks registered in the US during the fourth quarter, according to registration data that S&P Global Mobility provided to Bloomberg News. headline: SpaceX Was Buying Cybertrucks in Bulk. Tesla Didn’t Tell Anyone.
summary: Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) shareholders are about to start asking a pointed question: why did they have to find out from outside reporting that SpaceX was purchasing Cybertrucks in bulk, as Bloomberg is reporting?> Elon Musk controls both companies, which makes any commercial arrangement between them a textbook related-party transaction. Public companies are required to disclose material ... SpaceX Was Buying Cybertrucks in Bulk. Tesla Didn’t Tell Anyone.

headline: Latest News In Energy Transition - Statkraft Advances Wind Farm Repowering in Norway
summary: Statkraft has submitted its first licence application for the repowering of an existing wind farm in Norway, marking a significant step in the nation's energy transition efforts. The application, concerning the Hitra wind farm, involves the installation of fewer but more efficient turbines to nearly double the current electricity production while minimizing environmental impact. This repowering initiative aligns with the need for increased renewable energy production to support the energy...

headline: ‘What Happened to the Tesla Killers?’ Elon Musk Taunts, but One Rival CEO Is Playing Offense
summary: Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) CEO Elon Musk has publicly taunted rivals: “What happened to all the Tesla killers?” With Tesla holding over 54% of the U.S. electric vehicle (EV) market share in Q1 2026, the taunt carries weight. headline: Markets are finally broadening out in 2026. Is that a problem?
summary: US stock futures (ES=F, NQ=F, YM=F) edge higher Thursday morning after the S&P 500 (^GSPC) and Nasdaq Composite (^IXIC) closed yesterday's session at new record highs.  Morning Brief Anchor Julie Hyman and Yahoo Finance Head of News Myles Udland examine the year-to-date moves across sectors and how the market indexes are holding up compared to their equal-weight counterparts.

content.title: Markets are finally broadening out in 2026. Is that a problem?
content.description:
US stock futures (ES=F, NQ=F, YM=F) edge higher Thursday morning after the S&P 500 (^GSPC) and Nasdaq Composite (^IXIC) closed yesterday's session at new record highs.

Morning Brief Anchor Julie Hyman and Yahoo Finance Head of News Myles Udland examine the year-to-date moves across sectors and how the market indexes are holding up compared to their equal-weight counterparts.
content.summary: US stock futures (ES=F, NQ=F, YM=F) edge higher Thursday morning after the S&P 500 (^GSPC) and Nasdaq Composite (^IXIC) closed yesterday's session at new record highs.  Morning Brief Anchor Julie Hyman and Yahoo Finance Head of News Myles Udland examine the year-to-date moves across sectors and how the market indexes are holding up compared to their equal-weight counterparts.

headline: Bernstein Nearly Doubles Intel’s Price Target to $60: Has the Chipmaker’s Turnaround Finally Arrived?
summary: Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) stock just got a bold signal from Bernstein, raising its price target to $60 from $36 while maintaining a Market Perform rating. That’s nearly a doubling of the firm’s target, signaling growing conviction that Intel’s turnaround is gaining real traction. The move comes as INTC stock has already surged 81% year-to-date, fueled by ... Bernstein Nearly Doubles Intel’s Price Target to $60: Has the Chipmaker’s Turnaround Finally Arrived?

headline: Should You Buy, Sell or Hold Tesla Stock at $390?

headline: Tesla AI5 Chip Milestone Tests Robotic And Autonomy Ambitions
summary: Tesla, NasdaqGS:TSLA, has completed the tape out for its new AI5 chip, marking the final design phase for its latest in house AI processor. The company has also outlined progress on its follow up AI6 chip and Dojo3 system, highlighting a faster cadence in custom AI hardware development. Management framed these advances as core to future autonomy, robotics and data center products, including Optimus and potential autonomous services. Tesla's AI5 milestone arrives as the stock trades at...

headline: Can Tesla Stock Capitalize on the Market Rally Ahead of Earnings?
summary: Wall Street is looking for earnings per share of 36 cents, according to  FactSet  A year ago, Tesla reported first-quarter earnings of 27 cents.  EV deliveries and earnings, however, might not matter as much as what Musk and management say about AI-trained robo-taxis and robots.  At the closing bell on Thursday, Tesla stock was down 13% year to date and up 61% over the past 12 months.

content.title: Can Tesla Stock Capitalize on the Market Rally Ahead of Earnings?
content.summary: Wall Street is looking for earnings per share of 36 cents, according to  FactSet  A year ago, Tesla reported first-quarter earnings of 27 cents.  EV deliveries and earnings, however, might not matter as much as what Musk and management say about AI-trained robo-taxis and robots.  At the closing bell on Thursday, Tesla stock was down 13% year to date and up 61% over the past 12 months.

headline: Mining Stock Albemarle Breaks Out Amid Surging Lithium Prices
summary: ALB stock topped a buy point on Thursday and lithium stocks rallied broadly.  China's Ganfeng Lithium, one of the world's largest lithium producers, on Thursday reported a strong profit jump for the first quarter on demand for electric vehicles and energy storage systems.  The lithium cycle took a bullish turn in 2025 and continues to gain momentum in 2026.

content.title: Mining Stock Albemarle Breaks Out Amid Surging Lithium Prices
content.summary: ALB stock topped a buy point on Thursday and lithium stocks rallied broadly.  China's Ganfeng Lithium, one of the world's largest lithium producers, on Thursday reported a strong profit jump for the first quarter on demand for electric vehicles and energy storage systems.  The lithium cycle took a bullish turn in 2025 and continues to gain momentum in 2026.

headline: Nvidia is still an AI powerhouse as chip stocks rally in April
summary: Shares of chip giant Nvidia (NVDA) are on a tear in the month of April after a wobbly March. Great Hill Capital chairman Thomas Hayes joins Yahoo Finance Senior Reporters Brooke DiPalma and Ines Ferré to examine Nvidia's current position in the AI semiconductor space.

content.title: Nvidia is still an AI powerhouse as chip stocks rally in April
content.description:
Shares of chip giant Nvidia (NVDA) are on a tear in the month of April after a wobbly March.

Great Hill Capital chairman Thomas Hayes joins Yahoo Finance Senior Reporters Brooke DiPalma and Ines Ferré to examine Nvidia's current position in the AI semiconductor space.
content.summary: Shares of chip giant Nvidia (NVDA) are on a tear in the month of April after a wobbly March. Great Hill Capital chairman Thomas Hayes joins Yahoo Finance Senior Reporters Brooke DiPalma and Ines Ferré to examine Nvidia's current position in the AI semiconductor space.
